title: Specify user's gpg keyring for signing
body:
As I understand it, skopeo uses the current user's keyring for signing. When the tool is run using `sudo` this means the available signing keys are not mine but root's. As a user this is unexpected. I want to be able to use my private keys to sign, not root user's keys.

**Proposal:**
1. By default use the caller's keyring using a command like this:
   
   ```
   sudo gpg2 --list-keys --homedir $(eval echo ~$(who am i | awk '{print $1}'))/.gnupg
   ```
2. Add a `--keyringuser USERNAME` option to override default keyring lookup.

comments.body:
> Add a --keyringuser USERNAME option to override default keyring lookup.

I'm all for this one - proposal 1) is tricky (and we had hassles in docker/docker when playing with sudo and the user's home dir)

Does setting `$GNUPGHOME` work? The tests use that, so it should.

(Not saying that this is an optimal interface, just that there is a clumsy option right now.)
As a bigger hammer, `sudo -E` instead of plain `sudo`; though that may have unforeseen effects (root-owned files written into users’ home and the like.)

Actually just doing
sudo gpg2 --list-keys --homedir ~/.gnupg

Works
